feed.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions

entry.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
entry.summary:
In recent discussions on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, a notable point was raised about the mitigation techniques employed by Rootstock for SPV proofs. Specifically, this approach is encapsulated within their implementation of Partial Merkle Trees, as evidenced in the source code available on [GitHub](https://github.com/rsksmart/bitcoinj-thin/blob/f89816871d644436e34cd8d0a70c3c8ef41b0836/src/main/java/co/rsk/bitcoinj/core/PartialMerkleTree.javaL187). This method aligns with practices already deployed in existing systems, suggesting a standardization or commonality in handling SPV proofs across different platforms.
Additionally, further insights into the vulnerabilities associated with the design of Bitcoin's Merkle Tree have been shared by SDL in a detailed blog post, which can be accessed [here](https://bitslog.com/2018/06/09/leaf-node-weakness-in-bitcoin-merkle-tree-design). These references provide a deeper understanding of the intricacies involved in the cryptographic processes and underscore the importance of robust design to safeguard against potential weaknesses. By integrating these measures, there is an alignment with established systems, enhancing both security and efficiency in blockchain technologies.
